visa_free.notes: Full exemption from visa requirement per Libya e-visa official portal API (exemption_type: FULL). Classified as 'Exempted Nations' on evisa.gov.ly. No stay duration published in API. Wikipedia notes visa-free but independent travel still requires local sponsor or tour operator per Libya's general tourism rules.

Full exemption from visa requirement per Libya e-visa official portal API (exemption_type: FULL). Classified as 'Exempted Nations' on evisa.gov.ly. Wikipedia notes Algeria is visa-exempt but restricted to landing at Misurata and Tripoli Mitiga airports only; this landing restriction is not confirmed on official evisa.gov.ly API but is noted for caution.

Full exemption from visa requirement per Libya e-visa official portal API (exemption_type: FULL). Classified as 'Exempted Nations' on evisa.gov.ly. Wikipedia describes this agreement as of 'unclear current status'; the official portal API continues to classify Mauritania as FULL exempt.

Full exemption from visa requirement per Libya e-visa official portal API (exemption_type: FULL). Classified as 'Exempted Nations' on evisa.gov.ly. Wikipedia describes this agreement as of 'unclear current status'; the official portal API continues to classify Malaysia as FULL exempt.

Full exemption from visa requirement per Libya e-visa official portal API (exemption_type: FULL). Classified as 'Exempted Nations' on evisa.gov.ly. Wikipedia describes this agreement as of 'unclear current status'; the official portal API continues to classify Benin as FULL exempt.

e_visa.notes: E-visa available online via evisa.gov.ly (can_apply_online: true, exemption_type: NONE). Tourist e-visa: 30-day stay, 90-day validity from issuance, single entry, fee USD 63. All tourists must be escorted by licensed Libyan tourism officers; independent travel not permitted.

E-visa available online via evisa.gov.ly (can_apply_online: true, exemption_type: NONE). Tourist e-visa: 30-day stay, 90-day validity, single entry, fee USD 63. Not visa-free despite some third-party sources claiming otherwise; official API classifies Jordan as NONE exemption requiring e-visa.
E-visa available online via evisa.gov.ly (can_apply_online: true, exemption_type: NONE). Tourist e-visa: 30-day stay, 90-day validity, single entry, fee USD 63.

eta.notes: Partial exemption (exemption_type: PARTIAL, bypass_embassy_review: true) per Libya e-visa official portal. Wikipedia sources indicate the exemption covers Turkish nationals aged under 16 or over 55, who may enter without visa for up to 3 months; other age groups must apply online. The exact age ranges are from Wikipedia (not directly from official evisa.gov.ly UI text) and could not be independently confirmed on the official portal API response. Classified as ETA/partial exemption as the mechanism is age-based rather than a universal ordinary-passport grant.

visa_policy.notes: Libya operates an official e-visa portal at evisa.gov.ly (launched 21 March 2024). The portal API classifies countries into: FULL exemption (visa-free, 5 countries: Tunisia, Algeria, Mauritania, Malaysia, Benin), PARTIAL exemption (age-based, Turkey), can_apply_online=true (e-visa eligible, ~150+ countries), and cannot apply online (embassy only, 14 countries: Iran, Pakistan, Yemen, Egypt, Morocco, Iraq, Zaire/historical, Syria, Sudan, Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Nigeria, Chad, South Sudan). Tourist e-visa parameters: 30-day permitted stay, 90-day visa validity from issuance, single entry, fee USD 63. No visa-on-arrival system confirmed from official portal data. Israel is not listed on the official portal and entry is not permitted. All foreign tourists in Libya must be accompanied by licensed Libyan tourism officers for the duration of their stay; independent travel is not permitted. Countries with can_apply_online=false must obtain visas through Libyan diplomatic missions.

outbound_official.note: no official Libya government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Libyan government source. Libya is a conflict-affected state with no CBI program.

programs.name: Investor Residence Visa (Investment Promotion Law No. 9, 2010)
programs.notes: Article 12(7) of Investment Promotion Law No. 9 of 2010 (published by the Economic Development Authority at ejraat.gov.ly) grants approved foreign investors 'residence visas renewable for 5 years, the duration of the project, and multiple exit/re-entry visas'. No minimum investment amount is specified in the law text for this visa benefit; it applies to investment projects approved under the law. No path to permanent residence or citizenship is specified. Does not apply to oil and gas projects (Article 27). This is a residence benefit tied to an active investment project, not a standalone golden visa program.

data_quality.validation_notes: Validation run 2026-06-13. Changes vs prior record: (1) max_stay_days updated from null to 30 for all e_visa entries — confirmed by multiple cross-references to Libya e-visa portal: tourist e-visa is 30-day stay within a 90-day validity window, fee USD 63, single entry, source youngpioneertours.com corroborating official portal data; (2) Duplicate 'Burma'/'Myanmar' entry (both iso3=MMR) removed — consolidated as single 'Myanmar' entry; (3) Turkey ETA notes updated to include Wikipedia-sourced age ranges (under 16 or over 55 for visa-free access, up to 3 months) with caveat these age ranges are not directly confirmed on the official API response; (4) Algeria notes updated to flag the Wikipedia-reported landing restriction to Misurata and Tripoli Mitiga airports; (5) Jordan notes updated to clarify Jordan is classified as e_visa (exemption_type: NONE) in official API — some third-party sources incorrectly classify Jordan as visa-free but official evisa.gov.ly API is authoritative; (6) Turkey max_stay_days set to 90 (3-month stay per Wikipedia partial exemption); (7) Visa-free country notes updated for Mauritania, Malaysia, Benin to flag Wikipedia 'unclear agreement status' caveat while preserving API-authoritative FULL classification. CBI and RBI unchanged — no new official data found.
gaps: Maximum stay duration for visa-free nationals (Tunisia, Algeria, Mauritania, Malaysia, Benin) not published in official API or portal pages.

Turkey PARTIAL exemption age cutoffs (under 16 / over 55 giving 3-month access) sourced from Wikipedia, not directly from official evisa.gov.ly UI text or API response; cannot be fully confirmed from official Libyan government domain.

14 countries with embassy-only visas (Iran, Pakistan, Yemen, Egypt, Morocco, Iraq, Zaire/historical, Syria, Sudan, Afghanistan, Nigeria, Chad, Bangladesh, South Sudan): exact process, fees, and requirements not detailed on official portal.

Egypt has a conditional age/gender-based access rule per Wikipedia (male nationals under 18 or over 45, and all females, may enter visa-free) but this is not confirmed on any official Libyan government source (evisa.gov.ly, back.evisa.gov.ly, mofaicly.com); cannot be recorded in conditional_access without official source.
No official confirmation of Libya's freedom-of-movement agreements; Arab Maghreb Union and CEN-SAD protocols not operative/confirmed from official Libyan government pages.

RBI: Libya's Investment Promotion Law No. 9/2010 grants investors a 5-year renewable residence visa but no minimum investment amount is stated. No standalone golden visa or formal RBI program found on official sources.

Libya has two governing administrations (GNU in Tripoli and parallel administration in the east); the evisa.gov.ly portal appears to be the GNU (Tripoli-based) official portal. Status of entry through eastern Libya border crossings not covered.
mofaicly.com is the GNU Ministry of Foreign Affairs website but does not publish a visa exemption list directly.

Conditional access (Type A — held-credential waivers): No official Libyan government source publishes any rule granting easier entry to holders of US, Schengen, GCC, or other third-country visas or residence permits. Cannot be recorded without official source.

Conditional access (Type C — passport-type waivers): Wikipedia/IATA Timatic references bilateral diplomatic/service/official passport waivers for ~20 countries. However, the bilateral agreements are not published on any official Libyan government domain. Cannot be recorded in conditional_access without official treaty source.

E-visa fee of USD 63 and 30-day stay are confirmed by multiple travel operators referencing the evisa.gov.ly portal but the fee/duration are not directly extracted from the back.evisa.gov.ly/api/v1/countries API endpoint (which does not expose fee or duration fields).
Algeria landing restriction (Misurata and Tripoli Mitiga airports only) sourced from Wikipedia — not confirmed on official evisa.gov.ly.
